source.title: Selective processing of vestibular reafference during self-generated head motion.

confidence_rationale: High source quality (J Neurosci, single-unit in macaque, widely cited). Strong mapping fidelity: the paper directly demonstrates that the motor command (efference copy of neck activation), not proprioceptive feedback, is the effective input for cancelling self-generated vestibular reafference — directly analogous to SD-007's design decision that z_world_raw_prev (not z_self_prev) must drive the ReafferencePredictor. Transfer risk is moderate: this is the vestibular system, while SD-007 targets visual/optic flow, but the reafference cancellation principle is shown to be general across sensory modalities.
failure_signatures: Cancellation is gated by neck motor command activation — if no neck muscle activation occurs (e.g., arm-driven whole-body rotation), neurons encode head motion normally, suggesting the efference copy mechanism is specific to the activated musculature, not a global self-motion suppression.
Proprioceptive input alone does not produce the cancellation effect — passive stretch of neck muscles during externally driven head motion does not gate vestibular responses.

mapping.source_claim_statement: During active head-on-body movements, vestibular nucleus neurons are suppressed, and this suppression is mediated by efference copy of the neck motor command rather than by proprioceptive feedback or knowledge of self-generated head motion.
mapping.ree_translation: In SD-007, z_world_corrected = z_world_raw - ReafferencePredictor(z_world_raw_prev, a_prev). The choice of z_world_raw_prev as input (not z_self_prev) mirrors this finding: what matters for perspective correction is the content of the world stream as it responds to the agent's motor action, not the body-state representation alone. The neck motor command in the biological case corresponds to a_prev in REE; the vestibular nucleus suppression corresponds to the ReafferencePredictor output cancelling the action-induced change in z_world_raw.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The vestibular system cancels rotational/translational body-in-space signals; SD-007's ReafferencePredictor cancels visual perspective shift caused by agent locomotion in a gridworld. The computational principle is shared, but the specific sensory substrate and what constitutes 'self-caused' differs. In the biological case the motor command is directly available; in REE it is the discrete action token a_prev.
mapping.source_context: Alert rhesus macaques, in vivo single-unit recording from vestibular nucleus, active vs. passive head rotation paradigm.

confidence_components.notes: Transfer risk mainly from modality shift (vestibular -> visual optic flow) but the computational principle of efference-copy-based reafference cancellation is well-established across modalities by subsequent reviews.
